plants: A massive rosette of thick leaves that waits years to flower, then sends up a single towering stalk and dies.
Thick sword-shaped leaves arranged in a spiral, filled with gel that soothes burns and heals cuts.
Armored in spines and swollen with hoarded water, standing alone where nothing else will.
Its green stems do the work of leaves, having given up on foliage entirely to survive the drought.
Plump and fleshy, storing water in its leaves like a living canteen in a dry land.
Built for thirst, with every part reduced or hardened to hold onto whatever moisture it can find.
